user_query:
I'm preparing end-of-term materials for my Hartington history walks and school trips. Please use the files in this workspace to generate a concise tour summary and polish my email templates. Deliverables and constraints are below.

Inputs:
- config/report.json
- input/tour_feedback.csv
- input/messages.json
- scripts/report_generator.py

What to do:
1) Update configuration
   - Edit config/report.json so it contains these values:
     - report_title: "Hartington History Walks — Term Summary"
     - high_rating_threshold: 4.5
     - large_group_size: 20
     - highlight_routes: include "Village Heritage Walk", "Riverside Geology Stroll", and "Hartington Hall & Grounds" (keep as a JSON array)
   - The report you generate must reflect these config values in a visible "Configuration" section so I can cross-check.

2) Compute tour statistics and generate outputs
   - Modify scripts/report_generator.py so it reads the thresholds and title from config/report.json (do not hardcode). Use input/tour_feedback.csv as the only data source.
   - Compute and round to 2 decimals where appropriate:
     - Overview: total number of tours, total visitors (sum of group_size), average rating, average group size.
     - By Route: for each route, number of tours, average rating, total visitors, average group size.
     - By Month: number of tours per month (YYYY-MM) based on the date column.
     - Highlights: list all tours where rating >= high_rating_threshold OR group_size >= large_group_size. Show each as "YYYY-MM-DD — {route} (rating {rating}, group {group_size})".
     - Top Comment Keywords: compute the top 5 most frequent non-stopword terms from the comment field (case-insensitive; strip punctuation; singular/plural may be treated as-is). Exclude these stopwords at minimum: ["the","and","a","an","to","of","for","with","but","very","more","great","good","at","on","in","it","is","was","were","be","being","been","this","that","those","these","as","about"].
   - Write a Markdown report to output/docs/tour_report.md with sections in this order and headings exactly:
     - Title line using report_title from config
     - "Configuration" (list the high_rating_threshold, large_group_size, and highlight_routes values used)
     - "Overview"
     - "By Route" (table with columns: route | tours | avg_rating | total_visitors | avg_group_size)
     - "By Month" (table with columns: month | tours)
     - "Highlights" (bullet list)
     - "Top Comment Keywords" (bullet list showing term and count)
   - Also save a machine-readable CSV of the route-level stats to output/route_stats.csv with header: route,tours,avg_rating,total_visitors,avg_group_size.

3) Rewrite messages for tone, clarity, and brevity
   - Using input/messages.json, rewrite each draft to be friendly, professional, and concise for a school audience.
   - Preserve all placeholder tokens exactly as-is anywhere they appear: {DATE}, {TIME}, {MEETING_POINT}.
   - Constraints:
     - new_subject must be 60 characters or fewer.
     - new_body must be 120 words or fewer.
   - Save rewrites to output/messages_rewritten.json as an array of objects with fields: id, new_subject, new_body, word_count (where word_count is the number of words in new_body).

Verification notes:
- Your report must reflect the thresholds and title in config/report.json after you edit it, and include a Configuration section so I can verify values used.
- All outputs must be written exactly to:
  - output/docs/tour_report.md
  - output/route_stats.csv
  - output/messages_rewritten.json
- Use only the provided local inputs; no internet lookups.

Thank you! All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
